quiz.title: Unit 2 Quiz 4, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Subatomic Particles and Atomic Structure Calculations
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Subatomic Particle Properties, Atomic Number, Mass Number, Isotopes, Ions, Particle Discoveries, Mass Calculations

questions.question: Which particle was discovered by James Chadwick?
questions.explanation: James Chadwick discovered the neutron in 1932, finally explaining the missing mass in the nucleus and completing the modern picture of the atom.

questions.question: Which particle has approximately 1/1836 the mass of a proton?
questions.explanation: Electrons are extremely light compared to protons and neutrons, with a mass of about 1/1836 of a proton's mass (approximately 9.11 × 10⁻³¹ kg).

questions.question: Which combination gives the atomic number of an element?
questions.explanation: The atomic number (Z) is defined as the number of protons in the nucleus. This determines the element's identity.

questions.question: Which subatomic particle can change without changing the element?
questions.explanation: Neutrons can vary (creating isotopes) without changing the element because the number of protons remains the same. Changing protons would create a different element.

questions.question: Which particle determines the overall charge of an ion?
questions.explanation: The charge of an ion is determined by the difference between protons and electrons. Gaining or losing electrons creates ions while protons remain constant.

questions.question: Which particle can be found in energy levels or shells around the nucleus?
questions.explanation: Electrons occupy energy levels, shells, or orbitals outside the nucleus. Protons and neutrons are confined to the nucleus.

questions.question: Which particle is responsible for isotopic stability in the nucleus?
questions.explanation: Neutrons act as 'nuclear glue,' helping to stabilize the nucleus by reducing proton-proton repulsion. The neutron-to-proton ratio affects nuclear stability.

questions.question: A neutral atom has 17 protons and 18 neutrons. Its mass number is:
questions.explanation: Mass number = protons + neutrons = 17 + 18 = 35. This atom is chlorine-35.

questions.question: Which subatomic particle was discovered through oil drop experiments?
questions.explanation: Robert Millikan's oil drop experiment (1909) measured the charge of the electron, building on Thomson's discovery. While Thomson discovered electrons, Millikan precisely measured their charge.

questions.question: Which particle is fundamental to defining an element?
questions.explanation: The number of protons (atomic number) defines the element. All atoms of the same element have the same number of protons.

questions.question: Which particle explains the mass difference between isotopes?
questions.explanation: Isotopes have the same number of protons but different numbers of neutrons, which accounts for their different masses.

questions.question: Which particle is located outside the nucleus in orbitals?
questions.explanation: Electrons exist outside the nucleus in regions called orbitals or electron clouds. Protons and neutrons are inside the nucleus.

questions.question: Which particle contributes negligibly to the atom's mass?
questions.explanation: Electrons contribute very little to an atom's mass (about 1/1836 of a proton's mass). Nearly all atomic mass comes from protons and neutrons in the nucleus.

questions.question: If the mass number of a neutral atom is 23, and the number of protons is 11, what is the number of electrons?
questions.explanation: In a neutral atom, the number of electrons equals the number of protons. With 11 protons, a neutral atom has 11 electrons. This is sodium (Na).

questions.question: If the mass number of a neutral atom is 23, and the number of protons is 11, what is the number of neutrons?
questions.explanation: Mass number = protons + neutrons. So neutrons = mass number − protons = 23 − 11 = 12. This is sodium-23.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered atomic structure and calculations! ⚛️🧮
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review the relationships between protons, neutrons, and electrons.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on mass number calculations and particle properties.
scoreMessages.0: Please review subatomic particles, atomic number, mass number, and how to calculate electrons and neutrons.
